A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

(the planted bug):

2: ADD D 3
3: SWAP C D
4: IFZ B
5: SWAP B D

original routine yields, 'buggy' is what the routine above yields.

single line would also make every test pass, that line number is
accepted too.)

A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

A kiln-firing procedure is documented below.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.

Flags at the start:
- fire-core: COLD (values LIT/COLD)
- slip-ring: WHOLE (values WHOLE/CRACKED)
- soot-gate: LOWERED (values RAISED/LOWERED)
- ash-vent: BOUND (values FREE/BOUND)
- glaze-mark: UNSET (values SET/UNSET)
- damper: OPEN (values OPEN/SHUT)

The 12 steps:
- Slagturn: needs Veiling, fire-core=COLD, slip-ring=WHOLE; then ash-vent=FREE, damper=OPEN.
- Emberfast: needs Coiling, Kindlecall, fire-core=COLD; then fire-core=LIT, ash-vent=BOUND.
- Raking: needs Sealing, fire-core=COLD; then glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Wadding: needs Veiling, glaze-mark=SET, slip-ring=CRACKED; then ash-vent=BOUND, fire-core=COLD.
- Glossing: needs Ashdraw, ash-vent=BOUND, glaze-mark=UNSET; then glaze-mark=SET.
- Veiling: needs Ashdraw, Glossing, Quenching, damper=OPEN, fire-core=LIT; then slip-ring=CRACKED.
- Kindlecall: needs Wadding, ash-vent=BOUND, slip-ring=CRACKED; then slip-ring=WHOLE.
- Sealing: needs Glossing, Quenching, Veiling, ash-vent=BOUND; then glaze-mark=UNSET, damper=SHUT.
- Quenching: needs ash-vent=BOUND, damper=OPEN; then damper=SHUT, fire-core=LIT.
- Ashdraw: needs Quenching, fire-core=LIT, glaze-mark=UNSET, slip-ring=WHOLE; then damper=OPEN.
- Coiling: needs Glossing, Quenching, Sealing, ash-vent=BOUND, glaze-mark=UNSET, soot-gate=LOWERED; then glaze-mark=SET.
- Scrying: needs Quenching, ash-vent=BOUND; then damper=SHUT.

Steps done so far, in order: Quenching, Ashdraw, Glossing, Veiling, Wadding, Kindlecall, Sealing, Coiling, Slagturn, Raking, Emberfast.

SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

(the planted bug):

2: IFZ A
3: COPY A C
4: COPY A D
5: IFZ A
6: ZERO D
7: SUB A 9
8: SWAP B D
9: ZERO D
10: SUB A 4

Each test starts the registers as shown; 'correct' is what the
original routine yields, 'buggy' is what the routine above yields.

T1: start A=6 B=3 C=2 D=3 | correct A=0 B=0 C=11 D=0 | buggy A=0 B=0 C=11 D=0 -> pass
T2: start A=6 B=0 C=3 D=0 | correct A=0 B=0 C=12 D=0 | buggy A=0 B=0 C=12 D=0 -> pass
T3: start A=0 B=9 C=3 D=2 | correct A=0 B=2 C=12 D=0 | buggy A=0 B=0 C=12 D=0 -> FAIL

single line would also make every test pass, that line number is

A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

2. every gav becomes fole
3. drop after sabb
4. reverse all
5. swap first last

Evidence (input => ORIGINAL wheel output):
E1: plim plim fole plim ruv fole => plim ruv plim fole plim fole | shown wheel gives plim ruv plim fole

E3: ruv ruv ruv sabb sabb plim => ruv ruv sabb | shown wheel gives ruv ruv ruv sabb

Which rule number was corrupted? (If replacing a different single
rule could also make every evidence tape match, that number is
accepted too.)

A kiln-firing procedure is documented below.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.

Flags at the start:
- fire-core: LIT (values LIT/COLD)
- soot-gate: LOWERED (values RAISED/LOWERED)
- damper: OPEN (values OPEN/SHUT)
- ash-vent: BOUND (values FREE/BOUND)
- slip-ring: CRACKED (values WHOLE/CRACKED)
- glaze-mark: UNSET (values SET/UNSET)

The 12 steps:
- Ashdraw: needs Charmark, Emberfast, First Draw, slip-ring=WHOLE, soot-gate=LOWERED; then damper=SHUT.
- Raking: needs Coiling, Scrying, glaze-mark=SET; then damper=OPEN.
- Charmark: needs damper=OPEN, fire-core=LIT, glaze-mark=UNSET; then slip-ring=WHOLE, glaze-mark=SET.
- Kindlecall: needs First Draw, damper=SHUT; then fire-core=LIT.
- Coiling: needs Charmark, Scrying, damper=SHUT, fire-core=LIT, soot-gate=LOWERED; then fire-core=COLD, ash-vent=FREE.
- Veiling: needs Glossing, damper=OPEN, fire-core=COLD, glaze-mark=SET; then damper=SHUT.
- Cold Turning: needs Glossing, ash-vent=BOUND, damper=SHUT, slip-ring=CRACKED; then ash-vent=FREE, slip-ring=WHOLE.
- Emberfast: needs Charmark, ash-vent=BOUND, glaze-mark=SET, soot-gate=LOWERED; then ash-vent=FREE, glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Scrying: needs Ashdraw, Charmark, Emberfast, fire-core=COLD; then damper=OPEN.
- Sealing: needs Emberfast, Veiling, damper=SHUT; then slip-ring=CRACKED, ash-vent=BOUND.
- Glossing: needs Charmark, Emberfast, First Draw, fire-core=COLD, glaze-mark=UNSET, soot-gate=LOWERED; then glaze-mark=SET, damper=SHUT.
- First Draw: needs Charmark, damper=OPEN, fire-core=LIT, glaze-mark=SET; then slip-ring=WHOLE, fire-core=COLD.

Steps done so far, in order: Charmark, First Draw, Emberfast, Ashdraw, Scrying, Glossing, Kindlecall, Coiling, Raking, Veiling.

Exactly one step is legal to do next. Which one? Answer with the step name only.

SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

(the planted bug):

1: SUB B 8
2: ADD D 7
3: IFZ B
4: ZERO C
5: SUB D 1
6: SUB A 1

Each test starts the registers as shown; 'correct' is what the
original routine yields, 'buggy' is what the routine above yields.

T1: start A=9 B=0 C=1 D=1 | correct A=8 B=0 C=0 D=7 | buggy A=8 B=0 C=1 D=7 -> FAIL
T2: start A=4 B=0 C=8 D=5 | correct A=3 B=0 C=0 D=11 | buggy A=3 B=0 C=8 D=11 -> FAIL
T3: start A=0 B=6 C=9 D=0 | correct A=0 B=0 C=0 D=6 | buggy A=0 B=0 C=9 D=6 -> FAIL

Which line number holds the planted bug? (If replacing some other
single line would also make every test pass, that line number is
accepted too.)

11. If quillnut > 7: sell 3; else buy 2.

A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

A kiln-firing procedure is documented below.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.

Flags at the start:
- damper: OPEN (values OPEN/SHUT)
- fire-core: LIT (values LIT/COLD)
- slip-ring: WHOLE (values WHOLE/CRACKED)
- ash-vent: BOUND (values FREE/BOUND)
- soot-gate: RAISED (values RAISED/LOWERED)
- glaze-mark: UNSET (values SET/UNSET)

The 12 steps:
- Raking: needs ash-vent=BOUND, glaze-mark=UNSET, soot-gate=RAISED; then ash-vent=FREE, damper=SHUT.
- Slagturn: needs Coiling, Emberfast, Glossing, glaze-mark=SET; then ash-vent=BOUND, fire-core=COLD.
- Charmark: needs Emberfast, fire-core=LIT, soot-gate=RAISED; then soot-gate=LOWERED.
- Coiling: needs Ashdraw, Raking, glaze-mark=SET; then damper=OPEN.
- First Draw: needs Coiling, fire-core=COLD, glaze-mark=UNSET; then damper=OPEN, soot-gate=RAISED.
- Tamping: needs Glossing, ash-vent=BOUND, fire-core=LIT, soot-gate=LOWERED; then fire-core=COLD.
- Sealing: needs Glossing, Wadding, slip-ring=WHOLE, soot-gate=RAISED; then fire-core=LIT.
- Glossing: needs Raking, damper=SHUT, soot-gate=RAISED; then glaze-mark=SET.
- Ashdraw: needs Glossing, Raking, fire-core=LIT; then fire-core=COLD, glaze-mark=SET.
- Wadding: needs Emberfast, Glossing, Raking, ash-vent=BOUND, slip-ring=WHOLE; then damper=SHUT.
- Kindlecall: needs Slagturn, damper=SHUT, fire-core=COLD, slip-ring=WHOLE; then glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Emberfast: needs Ashdraw, Glossing, Raking, glaze-mark=SET, soot-gate=RAISED; then damper=OPEN, fire-core=LIT.

Steps done so far, in order: Raking, Glossing, Ashdraw, Emberfast, Coiling, Slagturn, Wadding, Sealing, Charmark.

Exactly one step is legal to do next. Which one? Answer with the step name only.

messages.content:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.

RULE <k>: <rule text> — replace rule k (e.g. RULE 2: every varn becomes ruv)
RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch

system_prompt:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
RULE <k>: <rule text> — replace rule k (e.g. RULE 2: every varn becomes ruv)
RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

initial_observation: TARGET: ro-za-ro-ti-ti-fen-ti. The gate accepts strings of exactly 7 glyphs. Probes left: 8. Turns left: 18.

messages.content:
You stand before a sealed glyph gate. It transforms any string of exactly 7 glyphs by one fixed hidden rule. The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za). The hidden rule chains TWO steps in order (the second step reads the result of the first); each step is one of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle; (e) if the first glyph is in a secret pair of glyphs the whole string rotates one step left, otherwise one step right.
Goal: feed the gate an input that it transforms into the TARGET string.
Actions (one per turn):
  PROBE <glyph-string>  - spend one probe; the gate prints the transformed string
  OPEN <glyph-string>   - final commit; you succeed only if the gate's output equals the TARGET
Glyph strings are dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro. Probes are limited; OPEN ends the episode.
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

initial_observation: The kiln is prepared. 0 of 10 steps done. Turn budget: 18.

messages.content:
You are performing a kiln-firing procedure, one action per turn.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.
- An illegal, repeated, unknown, or malformed action changes nothing and still costs the turn.
- Flag values are never shown after the start; track them yourself.
- Goal: complete all 10 steps within 18 turns.

Flags at the start:
- slip-ring: CRACKED (values WHOLE/CRACKED)
- glaze-mark: UNSET (values SET/UNSET)
- fire-core: COLD (values LIT/COLD)
- ash-vent: FREE (values FREE/BOUND)
- damper: SHUT (values OPEN/SHUT)

The 10 steps:
- Raking: needs Coiling, damper=OPEN, fire-core=LIT; then ash-vent=BOUND.
- Slagturn: needs Wadding, glaze-mark=SET; then slip-ring=WHOLE.
- Scrying: needs Charmark, Slagturn, ash-vent=FREE, slip-ring=WHOLE; then glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Wadding: needs Charmark, damper=OPEN; then glaze-mark=SET, damper=SHUT.
- Sealing: needs Coiling, Veiling, slip-ring=WHOLE; then fire-core=COLD, ash-vent=FREE.
- Coiling: needs Scrying, damper=OPEN; then fire-core=LIT, glaze-mark=SET.
- Veiling: needs Charmark, damper=OPEN, fire-core=LIT; then damper=SHUT, glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Charmark: needs fire-core=COLD; then damper=OPEN.
- Emberfast: needs Charmark, Slagturn, slip-ring=WHOLE; then slip-ring=CRACKED, damper=OPEN.
- Quenching: needs Scrying, Wadding, damper=OPEN, slip-ring=CRACKED; then slip-ring=WHOLE.

Action grammar: DO <StepName>

messages.content:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
RULE <k>: <rule text> — replace rule k (e.g. RULE 2: every varn becomes ruv)
RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

initial_observation: TARGET: fen-lus-ti-ro-ke-fen-gor. The gate accepts strings of exactly 7 glyphs. Probes left: 8. Turns left: 18.

messages.content:
You stand before a sealed glyph gate. It transforms any string of exactly 7 glyphs by one fixed hidden rule. The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za). The hidden rule chains TWO steps in order (the second step reads the result of the first); each step is one of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle; (e) if the first glyph is in a secret pair of glyphs the whole string rotates one step left, otherwise one step right.
Goal: feed the gate an input that it transforms into the TARGET string.
Actions (one per turn):
  PROBE <glyph-string>  - spend one probe; the gate prints the transformed string
  OPEN <glyph-string>   - final commit; you succeed only if the gate's output equals the TARGET
Glyph strings are dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro. Probes are limited; OPEN ends the episode.
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

system_prompt:
You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

Exactly one line of the routine was changed from the correct version. Each test starts the registers as shown and lists the finals the CORRECT routine yields. Repair the routine so every test passes, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
PATCH <line#> <instruction> — replace that line (e.g. PATCH 3 ADD B 2)
RUN — rerun all tests and report pass/fail
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

initial_observation: TARGET: ro-gor-fen-lus-ke. The gate accepts strings of exactly 5 glyphs. Probes left: 5. Turns left: 10.

messages.content:
You stand before a sealed glyph gate. It transforms any string of exactly 5 glyphs by one fixed hidden rule. The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za). The hidden rule is ONE of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle; (e) if the first glyph is in a secret pair of glyphs the whole string rotates one step left, otherwise one step right.
Goal: feed the gate an input that it transforms into the TARGET string.
Actions (one per turn):
  PROBE <glyph-string>  - spend one probe; the gate prints the transformed string
  OPEN <glyph-string>   - final commit; you succeed only if the gate's output equals the TARGET
Glyph strings are dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro. Probes are limited; OPEN ends the episode.
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

system_prompt:
You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

Exactly one line of the routine was changed from the correct version. Each test starts the registers as shown and lists the finals the CORRECT routine yields. Repair the routine so every test passes, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
PATCH <line#> <instruction> — replace that line (e.g. PATCH 3 ADD B 2)
RUN — rerun all tests and report pass/fail
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are exploring an underground burrow network of connected chambers.
Each turn you see only your current chamber and its exit directions; where an exit leads stays unknown until you walk through it, so remember what you discover.

messages.content:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
RULE <k>: <rule text> — replace rule k (e.g. RULE 2: every varn becomes ruv)
RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

Shown wheel:
1. every dree becomes nack
2. rotate right
3. rotate left
4. rotate left
5. rotate left
6. drop before ulm
7. drop after plim
Evidence (input => ORIGINAL output):
E1: ulm ulm yeg tosk dree nack nack plim => ulm yeg tosk nack nack nack plim

system_prompt:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
RULE <k>: <rule text> — replace rule k (e.g. RULE 2: every varn becomes ruv)
RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

messages.content:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
RULE <k>: <rule text> — replace rule k (e.g. RULE 2: every varn becomes ruv)
RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
RULE <k>: <rule text> — replace rule k (e.g. RULE 2: every varn becomes ruv)
RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

1. swap first last

E2: fole fole ulm yeg zim ulm => fole yeg
E3: ulm fole ulm zim yeg ulm => ulm yeg fole
Shown wheel now gives: E1 match, E2 match, E3 MISMATCH

system_prompt:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
RULE <k>: <rule text> — replace rule k (e.g. RULE 2: every varn becomes ruv)
RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

initial_observation: The kiln is prepared. 0 of 7 steps done. Turn budget: 10.

messages.content:
You are performing a kiln-firing procedure, one action per turn.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.
- An illegal, repeated, unknown, or malformed action changes nothing and still costs the turn.
- Flag values are never shown after the start; track them yourself.
- Goal: complete all 7 steps within 10 turns.

Flags at the start:
- glaze-mark: SET (values SET/UNSET)
- damper: OPEN (values OPEN/SHUT)
- fire-core: LIT (values LIT/COLD)

The 7 steps:
- Sealing: needs Glossing, Kindlecall, fire-core=COLD, glaze-mark=UNSET; then glaze-mark=SET.
- Glossing: needs Ashdraw, Coiling, fire-core=LIT, glaze-mark=SET; then fire-core=COLD, damper=SHUT.
- Slagturn: needs Coiling, fire-core=COLD, glaze-mark=UNSET; then damper=OPEN.
- Kindlecall: needs damper=SHUT, fire-core=COLD; then damper=OPEN.

initial_observation: TARGET: gor-ro-mu-lus-ti. The gate accepts strings of exactly 5 glyphs. Probes left: 5. Turns left: 10.

messages.content:
You stand before a sealed glyph gate. It transforms any string of exactly 5 glyphs by one fixed hidden rule. The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za). The hidden rule is ONE of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle; (e) if the first glyph is in a secret pair of glyphs the whole string rotates one step left, otherwise one step right.
Goal: feed the gate an input that it transforms into the TARGET string.
Actions (one per turn):
  PROBE <glyph-string>  - spend one probe; the gate prints the transformed string
  OPEN <glyph-string>   - final commit; you succeed only if the gate's output equals the TARGET
Glyph strings are dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro. Probes are limited; OPEN ends the episode.
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

The gate prints: ke-ro-mu-lus-za. Probes left: 1. Turns left: 6.

The gate prints: mu-lus-za-ke-ro. Probes left: 0. Turns left: 5.
